channel.title: Entertainment News | CableTV.com
channel.description: Super fans, rejoice! Our entertainment news keeps you passionate about sports, TV, and streaming.

item.title: Fubo Lands Channel for Championship MLB Team
item.dc:creator: CableTV.com
item.description: Fubo just landed a deal with Los Angeles Dodgers-specific channel SportsNet LA. Here’s what it means for Dodgers fans, and how they can start watching.
item.content:encoded:
Fubo and SportsNet LA sign agreement

Fubo, a sports-centric live TV streaming service, signed a carriage agreement with SportsNet LA, the company announced Thursday. It marks the first time the streaming service has been able to distribute the Dodgers-centric channel. Customers need at least the Fubo base plan to get access to SportsNet LA, the company said in a news release. Previously, SportsNet LA was available to in-market fans through cable and satellite providers or via its standalone streaming service, SNLA+. Now Fubo subscribers can watch most Dodgers games live, plus postgame shows and original content such as “Backstage: Dodgers.” The Dodgers start their season Thursday, March 26, at home against the Arizona Diamondbacks.

Getting SportsNet LA is a big win for Fubo

Fubo is known for its large offering of live sports. But that reputation has taken somewhat of a hit after its dispute with NBCUniversal, which caused the company to lose access to every NBC regional sports network and other channels that carried live sports like TNT and TBS. But getting the Dodgers just before Opening Day gives Fubo some of its groove back. The Dodgers have won back-to-back World Series titles and are expected to make another deep postseason run. Whether customers who left return to Fubo remains to be seen. But if there are any Dodgers fans who have wanted to try Fubo but were turned off by the lack of SportsNet LA access, this is the time to try it.

Who we are

At CableTV.com, we’re not just entertainment experts—we’re also fans just like you. So while our first priority is reporting the best and most accurate information about TV providers and streaming services, we also love talking about our favorite shows and movies. So you can count on us to share our honest opinions and unbridled enthusiasm. For more on our process, check out our Editorial Policy.

More industry news for sports fans

  • Spectrum Unveils Clutch TV Feature for March Madness
  • Soccer Viewership Is Exploding Ahead of the 2026 World Cup: Is Your TV Setup Ready?
  • Spectrum, Cox Merger Confirmed By FCC: What It Means For Customers

item.title: What Channel Is Comedy Central On?
item.dc:creator: CableTV.com
item.description: Find Comedy Central channel numbers for Xfinity, DIRECTV, and Spectrum, plus the best ways to stream Comedy Central on Philo or Hulu + Live TV in March 2026.
item.content:encoded:
Cable network Comedy Central launched on April 1, 1991—April Fool’s Day, appropriately. Its early-days programming included the first incarnations of “The Daily Show” and “Politically Incorrect with Bill Maher,” as well as cult favorites “Mystery Science Theater 3000” and the animated “Dr. Katz, Professional Therapist.” It wasn’t until the late ’90s that Comedy Central broke into the mainstream with the introductions of new “Daily Show” host Jon Stewart and the foul-mouthed, animated “South Park.” Those two shows remain Comedy Central’s flagship originals today, along with around-the-clock comedic movies and TV reruns, plus stand-up comedy specials. Comedy Central’s current lineup of original shows is smaller than it used to be—it’s essentially just “The Daily Show” and “South Park”—but the Paramount-owned channel is still a reliable go-to for laughs at any time of the day. It’s also available on most cable, satellite, and live TV streaming services.
Jump to:
  • Comedy Central channel numbers
  • Get Comedy Central with cable or satellite
  • Get Comedy Central with a live TV streaming service
  • Get Comedy Central through the streaming app

Search for TV providers with Comedy Central in your area

Enter your zip code below to see top providers in your neighborhood.

What channel is Comedy Central on near me?

Back in the beginning, Comedy Central wasn’t an easy channel to find; word-of-mouth movements and letter-writing campaigns had to bombard regional cable companies to get the network added. It’s a different story today, as Comedy Central is as readily available as Paramount cousins like MTV and VH1. Below are 50+ cities and the TV provider channels where you can find Comedy Central.

Comedy Central channels in major US cities

City Cox channel DIRECTV channel DISH channel Optimum channel Spectrum channel Xfinity channel
Albuquerque, NM — 249 107 — — 48
Anchorage, AK — 249 107 — — —
Atlanta, GA 37 249 107 — 66 68
Austin, TX — 249 107 95 113 —
Bakersfield, CA — 249 107 — 37 —
Baltimore, MD — 249 107 — — 64
Boston, MA — 249 107 — — 76
Bronx, NY — 249 107 50 45 —
Brooklyn, NY — 249 107 50 45 —
Buffalo, NY — 249 107 — 46 —
Charlotte, NC — 249 107 — 52 —
Chicago, IL — 249 107 — —
Cincinnati, OH — 249 107 — 50 —
Cleveland, OH — 249 107 — 50 —
Colorado Springs, CO — 249 107 — — 62
Columbus, OH — 249 107 — 35 —
Dallas, TX — 249 107 — 81 —
Denver, CO — 249 107 — — 72
Detroit, MI — 249 107 — — 62
El Paso, TX — 249 107 — 42 —
Fort Lauderdale, FL — 249 107 — —
Fort Worth, TX — 249 107 — 81 —
Fresno, CA — 249 107 — — 31
Honolulu, HI — 249 107 — — —
Houston, TX — 249 107 — 59 59
Indianapolis, IN — 249 107 — 25 35
Jacksonville, FL — 249 107 — — 60
Kansas City, MO — 249 107 — 37 —
Las Vegas, NV 56 249 107 — — —
Los Angeles, CA — 249 107 — 64 —
Louisville, KY — 249 107 — 148 —
Memphis, TN — 249 107 — — 60
Miami, FL — 249 107 — — 67
Milwaukee, WI — 249 107 — 54 —
Minneapolis, MN — 249 107 — — 31
Nashville, TN — 249 107 — 65 60
New Orleans, LA 39 249 107 — — —
New York, NY — 249 107 — 45 —
Oklahoma City, OK 56 249 107 95 81 —
Orlando, FL — 249 107 — 66 73
Philadelphia, PA — 249 107 — — 22
Phoenix, AZ 51 249 107 — — —
Pittsburgh, PA — 249 107 — — 49
Portland, OR — 249 107 — 50 60
Sacramento, CA — 249 107 — — 55
Salt Lake City, UT — 249 107 — — 58
San Antonio, TX — 249 107 — 48 —
San Diego, CA 48 249 107 — 60 —
San Francisco, CA — 249 107 — — 63
San Jose, CA — 249 107 — — 63
Seattle, WA — 249 107 — — 60
St. Louis, MO — 249 107 — 69 —
St. Paul, MN — 249 107 — 66 —
Tampa, FL — 249 107 — 61 70
Tucson, AZ 46 249 107 — — 39
Washington, DC — 249 107 — — 68

Get Comedy Central with cable or satellite

You can find Comedy Central in most cable TV lineups under various channel numbers; it’s more consistently numbered on satellite TV services DIRECTV and DISH.

Provider Type Price Comedy Central Details
Cox Cable $20.00–$184.00/mo. ✔ View Plan

Read Cox review
DIRECTV Satellite $89.99–$169.99/mo. ✔ View Plan

Read DIRECTV review
DISH Satellite $89.99–$119.99/mo. ✔ View Plan

Read DISH review
Optimum Cable $15.00–$140.00/mo. ✔ View Plan

Read Optimum review
Spectrum Cable $45.00–$160.00/mo. ✔ View Plan

Read Spectrum review
Verizon Fios Cable $105.00–$149.00/mo. ✔ View Plan

Read Verizon Fios review
Xfinity Cable $55.00–$125.00/mo. ✔ View Plan

Read Xfinity review

Get Comedy Central with a live TV streaming service

Just like with cable and satellite, Comedy Central is available on all major live TV streaming services, including YouTube TV and Hulu + Live TV.

Provider Type Price Comedy Central Details
DIRECTV Streaming $89.99–$169.99/mo. ✔ View Plan

Read DIRECTV review
Fubo Streaming $14.99–$94.99/mo. ✔ View Plan

Read Fubo review
Hulu + Live TV Streaming $88.99–$99.99/mo. ✔ View Plan

Read Hulu + Live TV review
Philo Streaming $33.00/mo. ✔ View Plan

Read Philo review
Sling TV Streaming $19.99–$60.99/mo. ✔ View Plan

Read Sling TV review
YouTube TV Streaming $82.99/mo. ✔ View Plan

Read YouTube TV review

Get Comedy Central with a streaming app

There’s no dedicated Comedy Central streaming app available for Roku, Amazon Fire TV, or other streaming platforms anymore—but there is Paramount+, which features nearly all of CC’s original programming, both old and new, on-demand.

What’s on Comedy Central?

Comedy Central’s current lineup of ongoing original TV shows consists of “The Daily Show” and “South Park,” as well as the new “Beavis & Butt-Head” reboot, which began on Paramount+ in 2022. The rest of Comedy Central’s schedule consists of theatrical-release comedy movies and reruns of “The Office,” “Parks & Recreation,” “Seinfeld,” “Family Guy,” and “Reno 911!”

Comedy Central channel guide FAQ

item.title: Crunchyroll Finally Announced Its Spring 2026 Anime Lineup, and It’s a Doozy
item.dc:creator: CableTV.com
item.description: Your Spring 2026 anime guide is here! See the full Crunchyroll lineup, including premiere dates for every new show and returning favorite this season.
item.content:encoded:
What’s new on Crunchyroll this month?

The Winter anime season is just wrapping up with the “JUJUTSU KAISEN,” “Frieren: Beyond Journey’s End,” and “TRIGUN STARGAZE” season finales this week. But you won’t have to wait long for new anime. Crunchyroll announced its Spring 2026 lineup on Tuesday afternoon. It’s looking like this will be another great lineup, with new seasons of “Dorohedoro” and “Welcome to Demon School! Iruma-kun” and the giant-sized premiere of “ONE PIECE”‘s Elbaph arc. You can also look forward to some new manga adaptations, like “Witch Hat Atelier” and “Go For It, Nakamura-kun!!”, the latter of which became a popular art meme in 2021. Feel free to browse the full schedule below or visit our Crunchyroll schedule page for the latest updates. And then come back at the end of the month when we update our full Anime Simulcast Schedule—featuring recommendations and trailers from every major anime streaming service.

March 28

  • “Agents of the Four Seasons: Dance of Spring” (WIT Studio)

March 31

  • “The Food Diary of Miss Maid” (EMT Squared and Magic Bus)
  • “The strongest job is apparently not a hero or a sage, but an appraiser (provisional)!” (Studio Flad)

April 1

  • “Dorohedoro” Season 2 (MAPPA)
  • “Go For It, Nakamura-kun!!” (Drive)
  • “The Beginning After the End” Season 2 (A-CAT)
  • “Reborn as a Vending Machine, I Now Wander the Dungeon” Season 3 (Studio Gokumi and dAxsiZ)
  • “Classroom of the Elite 4th Season: Second Year, First Semester” (Lerche)

April 2

  • “Dr. STONE SCIENCE FUTURE” Cour 3 (TMS Entertainment)

April 3

  • “That Time I Got Reincarnated as a Slime” Season 4 (8bit)
  • “SNOWBALL EARTH” (Studio KAI)
  • “The Angel Next Door Spoils Me Rotten 2” (project No.9)

April 4

  • “Daemons of the Shadow Realm” (bones film)
  • “NEEDY GIRL OVERDOSE” (Yostar Pictures)
  • “Welcome to Demon School! Iruma-kun” Season 4 (Bandai Namco Pictures)
  • “Ascendance of a Bookworm: Adopted Daughter of an Archduke” (WIT Studio)
  • “Kusunoki’s Garden of Gods” (JUVENAGE)
  • “Mistress Kanan is Devilishly Easy” (Studio KAI)

April 5

  • “ONE PIECE” Elbaph Arc (Toei Animation)
  • “GHOST CONCERT : missing Songs” (ENGI)

April 6

  • “Witch Hat Atelier” (BUG FILMS)
  • “LIAR GAME” (MADHOUSE)
  • “An Observation Log of My Fiancée Who Calls Herself a Villainess” (Ashi Production)
  • “The Klutzy Class Monitor and the Girl with the Short Skirt” (Zero-G)

April 7

  • “MARRIAGETOXIN” (bones film)
  • “Eren the Southpaw” (Signal.MD and Production I.G)
  • “Even a Replica Can Fall in Love” (Voil)
  • “I Made Friends with the Second Prettiest Girl in My Class” (CONNECT)

April 8

  • “Re:ZERO -Starting life in Another World-” Season 4 (WHITE FOX)
  • “Rent-a-Girlfriend” Season 5 (TMS Entertainment)
  • “Gals Can’t Be Kind to Otaku!?” (TMS Entertainment)

April 9

  • “A Hundred Scenes of AWAJIMA” (MADHOUSE)
  • “Kujima: Why Sing, When You Can Warble?” (Studio Hibari)
  • “The Warrior Princess and the Barbaric King” (Jumondou)

April 10

  • “The Drops of God” (Satelight)
  • “Botan Kamiina Fully Blossoms When Drunk” (Soigne)

April 12

  • “Wistoria: Wand and Sword” Season 2 (Actas and Bandai Namco Pictures)

April 14

  • “I Want to End This Love Game” (FelixFilm)

Who we are

At CableTV.com, we’re not just entertainment experts—we’re also fans just like you. So while our first priority is reporting the best and most accurate information about TV providers and streaming services, we also love talking about our favorite shows and movies. So you can count on us to share our honest opinions and unbridled enthusiasm. For more on our process, check out our Editorial Policy.

More industry news for TV fans

  • “Sonic 4” Release Date
  • “Witch Hat Atelier” Release Date
  • “The Super Mario Galaxy Movie” Finds Its Yoshi

item.title: This Fantasy Series Is Finally Getting an Anime, and You Can Watch It Early
item.dc:creator: CableTV.com
item.description: When does the ‘Witch Hat Atelier’ anime come to streaming? Let’s talk premiere date, English trailer, and more details for anime super-fans!
item.content:encoded:
Originally published 3/13/26, updated 3/26/26

A world of witches and magic

If you’re a fan of fantasy manga, you might be familiar with “Witch Hat Atelier.” It’s a very popular story about an apprentice witch in a world where rune-based magic is a carefully guarded secret, practiced only by witches who wear distinctive pointy hats. The original manga started publication in 2016, and the anime adaptation was actually supposed to come out in 2025. However, production delays pushed that date to 2026, and now the long-anticipated “Witch Hat Atelier” adaptation is finally coming out this April. Let’s talk about the “Witch Hat Atelier” premiere date—and how you might be able to watch it early (if you’re near Seattle).

When is the “Witch Hat Atelier” premiere date?

”Witch Hat Atelier” will premiere on Crunchyroll on April 6 at 10:00 a.m. ET. The first two episodes will premiere back-to-back, and both will have an English dub available on release. The dub is produced by Crunchyroll and will feature the voice talents of Anjali Kunapaneni as Coco and Joshua A. Waters as Qifrey.

How to watch “Witch Hat Atelier” early

Anime fans attending Sakura-Con will be able to catch the first two episodes early on April 4 at 10:30 a.m. PT in the Seattle Convention Center’s Ballroom 2. Crunchyroll will also be screening several other anime previews in Ballroom 2 as part of its Crunchyroll Presents series. Other titles include “Classroom of the Elite,” “Dr. STONE SCIENCE FUTURE,” and “LIAR GAME.” But we East Coasters will have to wait for the anime season to officially start. Wondering when each title will premiere on streaming? Check back later in the month for our full Anime Simulcast Schedule, featuring premieres from Crunchyroll, Netflix, Hulu, and more.

Who we are

At CableTV.com, we’re not just entertainment experts—we’re also fans just like you. So while our first priority is reporting the best and most accurate information about TV providers and streaming services, we also love talking about our favorite shows and movies. So you can count on us to share our honest opinions and unbridled enthusiasm. For more on our process, check out our Editorial Policy.

More industry news for TV fans

  • Upcoming Anime Simulcasts
  • New Yoshi Voice Actor Revealed
  • Watch ‘One Piece’ Season 2

item.title: ‘Bridgerton’ Season 2 Cast: Where Are They Now?
item.dc:creator: CableTV.com
item.description: Where are the stars of the Ton now? See the full “Bridgerton” Season 2 cast list plus expert 2026 updates on Jonathan Bailey, Simone Ashley, and more!
item.content:encoded:
✦ Edited and fact-checked by Randy Harward, our Entertainment Editor with 25 years covering entertainment and TV.
The second season of “Bridgerton” didn’t just break records—it launched its stars into the Hollywood stratosphere. After debuting on March 25, 2022, the season became the most-watched English-language series on Netflix at the time, amassing over 656 million hours viewed in its first month. While the enemies-to-lovers tension between Anthony Bridgerton and Kate Sharma defined the season, the cast’s real-world trajectory since then has been just as dramatic. From blockbuster Marvel roles to intense thrillers, here is what the stars of Season 2 are doing in 2026.

The official “Bridgerton” Season 2 trailer

But first … relive the “Capital R Rake” era with the OG trailer:

“Bridgerton” Season 2 cast: Where are they now? (2026 update)

The Season 2 blockbuster cast has been busy, to say the least. Here are some of the biggest projects from its biggest stars.

Jonathan Bailey (Anthony Bridgerton)

Since his turn as the stubborn Viscount, Bailey has become a bona fide blockbuster lead. In 2025, he starred alongside Scarlett Johansson in “Jurassic World Rebirth” and captivated audiences as Prince Fiyero (The Scarecrow) in the “Wicked” film franchise.

Simone Ashley (Kate Sharma)

The Viscountess has moved from The Ton to the high-stakes world of fashion and psychological thrillers. Ashley starred in the 2025 mystery “This Tempting Madness” and joined an all-star cast for the 2026 sequel “The Devil Wears Prada 2.”

Nicola Coughlan (Penelope Featherington)

Coughlan has balanced her role as Penelope Bridgerton with major film and television projects. She starred in the 2024 historical comedy “Seize Them!” and the acclaimed series “Big Mood.”

Luke Newton (Colin Bridgerton)

Newton is exploring the sci-fi genre, taking on a lead role in the thriller “White Mars” (2026) alongside Lucy Hale. He also received praise for his stage work in the 2025 play “House of McQueen.”

Charithra Chandran (Edwina Sharma)

Chandran has joined one of Netflix’s most massive franchises. In 2026, she debuted as Princess Nefertari Vivi in the second season of the live-action “One Piece” adaptation.

Claudia Jessie (Eloise Bridgerton)

Jessie has tackled serious dramatic roles, including the Netflix miniseries “Toxic Town” (2025), which explores the true story of the Corby poisonings.

Complete “Bridgerton” Season 2 cast list

Based on official records, here is the full ensemble that brought the second season to life:

Main cast

  • Adjoa Andoh as Lady Agatha Danbury
  • Lorraine Ashbourne as Mrs. Varley
  • Simone Ashley as Kate Sharma Bridgerton
  • Jonathan Bailey as Anthony Bridgerton
  • Ruby Barker as Marina Crane (guest)
  • Harriet Cains as Philippa Featherington Finch
  • Bessie Carter as Prudence Featherington Dankworth
  • Charithra Chandran as Edwina Sharma
  • Shelley Conn as Lady Mary Sharma
  • Nicola Coughlan as Penelope Featherington Bridgerton
  • Phoebe Dynevor as Daphne Basset Bridgerton
  • Ruth Gemmell as Lady Violet Bridgerton
  • Florence Hunt as Hyacinth Bridgerton
  • Martins Imhangbe as Will Mondrich
  • Claudia Jessie as Eloise Bridgerton
  • Calam Lynch as Theo Sharpe
  • Luke Newton as Colin Bridgerton
  • Golda Rosheuvel as Queen Charlotte
  • Luke Thompson as Benedict Bridgerton
  • Will Tilston as Gregory Bridgerton
  • Polly Walker as Lady Portia Featherington
  • Rupert Young as Jack Featherington
  • Julie Andrews as the voice of Lady Whistledown

Notable recurring guest stars

  • Joanana Bobin as Lady Araminta Cowper
  • Dominic Coleman as Lord Cowper
  • Ruby Stokes as Francesca Bridgerton
  • Jessica Madsen as Lady Cressida Cowper Gun
  • Kathryn Drysdale as Genevieve Delacroix
  • Hugh Sachs as Brimsley
  • Emma Naomi as Alice Mondrich
  • James Fleet as King George III

What to binge next

  • ‘Bridgerton’ Breaks the Mold With Season 5 Leading Couple
  • Shows and Movies Like ‘Bridgerton’
  • New ‘One Piece’ Season 2 Clip Is a Fan Favorite
CableTV.com editors created this article using original analysis and proprietary inputs, with AI tools assisting in drafting. The final content was reviewed and edited for accuracy and quality.

item.title: Dropout Has a New Animated Series, and You Can Watch the First Episode Right Now
item.dc:creator: CableTV.com
item.description: Original animation has returned to Dropout! Watch the “Toonout” premiere, learn how it fits into the indie animation boom, and see what’s next for the series.
item.content:encoded:
“The Legend of Legendarius” is the perfect opening act for Dropout’s animated anthology

Dropout, the comedy streaming service from the group formerly known as CollegeHumor, is taking a chance on independent animated shorts. Its new anthology series “Toonout” premiered Tuesday night, and it’s already hit the ground running. The first short, streaming now, is called “The Legend of Legendarius.” It was storyboarded, animated, and directed entirely by Raj Brueggemann, a storyboard artist on “Big City Greens.” (You might recognize some of that Disney Channel art style in the character design.) “The Legend of Legendarius” also features the voices of Katie Marovich and Ross Bryant, who have often appeared in old-school CollegeHumor and newer Dropout projects. It’s a fantastic short, from its story to its voice cast, and wouldn’t feel out of place on Disney Channel or Cartoon Network between longer shows. I mean, except for the swearing. You can watch a short preview of the episode below, or subscribe to Dropout to watch the full four-minute episode.

Indie animation is getting bigger

It’s been a rough time for the animation industry. Don’t believe me? Just take a look at any of Jellybox’s “Umbert Actually!” shorts, featuring CollegeHumor alum Adam Conover. The industry is shrinking, and that means there are hordes of talented animators with no projects to work on. Without the support of big studios like Warner Bros., workers turned to independent animation. We’ve seen an unprecedented wave of successful indie projects lately, including “Helluva Boss,” “The Amazing Digital Circus,” and “Bigtop Burger.” Indie animation also has the benefit of dodging the infamously strict kids’ TV censors. It really fights back against the idea that all animation has to either be family friendly or an edgy adult comedy like “South Park.” Indies can fall in the middle—utilizing the gorgeous character design, shape language, and worldbuilding of kids TV while still being able to depict adult themes. So “Legendarius” can live in a place called “Helinearth” and still have an appealing, Disney Channel-esque character design. He can cause sexual identity crises in his fans without execs pearl-clutching about a background character possibly being gay. His squishy, adorable little son can use swear words. And there’s nothing The Mouse can do about it.

What’s next for “Toonout”?

Assuming one animated short per animator, there are still five shorts to go in Season 1 of “Toonout.” Here’s the full list of animators whose work you’ll see this season:

  • Raj Brueggemann (“Big City Greens,” “Twelve Forever”)
  • Victor Courtright (“Thundercats Roar,” “Aquaman: King of Atlantis”)
  • Jonathan Wallach (“Big City Greens”)
  • Kay Hayes (“Monsters at Work,” “Rapunzel’s Tangled Adventure”)
  • Violaine Briat (“Spider-Man: Across the Spider-Verse”)
  • Matt Braly (“Amphibia”)

If “Toonout” does well on the platform, Dropout might renew it for a second season. I’d love to see what an even wider net of animators can come up with. Maybe something from the Drawfee crew or “Game Changer” alum Kiana Khansmith? And with Dropout’s recent foray into licensing, I wonder if we might see Dropout host full shows from indie studios like Glitch Productions, Spindlehorse/Spindleroo, or Matt Braly’s recently launched studio Fantasy Project.

Who we are

At CableTV.com, we’re not just entertainment experts—we’re also fans just like you. So while our first priority is reporting the best and most accurate information about TV providers and streaming services, we also love talking about our favorite shows and movies. So you can count on us to share our honest opinions and unbridled enthusiasm. For more on our process, check out our Editorial Policy.

More industry news for TV fans

  • ‘Don’t Hug Me I’m Scared’ on Dropout
  • ‘Bridgerton’ Announces Season 5 Couple
  • ‘The Wheel of Time’ Returns With Animated Series

item.title: ‘Sonic 4’ Just Found Its Amy Rose and a Release Date
item.dc:creator: CableTV.com
item.description: Sonic 4 hits theaters March 19, 2027! Get the full trailer breakdown, cast reveals like Kristen Bell as Amy, and expert news only on CableTV.com.
item.content:encoded:
The countdown is on: “Sonic 4” comes out in less than a year

The Sonic the Hedgehog franchise has pulled in a lot of golden rings at the box office, with the third installment crossing nearly $500 million and proving that audiences haven’t tired of the blue blur. After the dramatic introduction of Keanu Reeves as Shadow, fans have been desperate to know if the series would continue its hot streak. The wait for confirmation is over. Following the title reveal teaser that dropped on March 19, 2026, we now have a firm window for the next adventure. And while James Marsden (our favorite Donut Lord) is back, he’s bringing some heavy hitters from “Nobody Wants This,” “Parks and Recreation,” and “The IT Crowd” with him. Instead of just one rival, the fourth film looks to be diving deep into the Sonic CD lore, pitting Sonic against his most dangerous mechanical mirror yet. Here is everything you need to know about the release date, the star-studded new cast, and those game-changing post-credits teases.

What is “Sonic the Hedgehog 4” about?

While official plot summaries are firmly under wraps, the first teaser and the “Sonic 3” post-credits scenes have painted a clear picture. The film will introduce Metal Sonic, a cold, silent, and deadly AI replica of Sonic who believes he is the real version. The story also brings in Amy Rose (voiced by Kristen Bell), the fan-favorite pink hedgehog, who was teased saving Sonic from a legion of “Metals” in a futuristic forest setting. Fans are already theorizing a time-travel plot inspired by the 1993 classic Sonic CD, which could see Sonic running so fast he enters a bad future ruled by Dr. Robotnik. Speaking of the Doctor … yes, the trailer confirms that Jim Carrey is once again defying retirement to return as the villainous Eggman.

“Sonic the Hedgehog 4” release date

Paramount has officially scheduled “Sonic 4” to hit theaters on Friday, March 19, 2027. This follows the franchise’s successful trend of spring and holiday releases, giving the production team a full year of principal photography, which began this month.

“Sonic the Hedgehog 4” cast highlights

The countdown to chaos includes an incredible lineup of new voices and returning legends:

  • Ben Schwartz: Sonic the Hedgehog
  • Kristen Bell: Amy Rose (newly confirmed!)
  • Keanu Reeves: Shadow the Hedgehog
  • Idris Elba: Knuckles the Echidna
  • Colleen O’Shaughnessey: Miles “Tails” Prower
  • Jim Carrey: Dr. Ivo Robotnik
  • Nick Offerman: Role undisclosed (A “Parks & Rec” reunion with Schwartz!)
  • Ben Kingsley: Role undisclosed
  • Matt Berry and Richard Ayoade: Roles undisclosed (“The IT Crowd” reunion!)

How to watch the Sonic franchise

If you need to catch up on the Chaos Emerald saga before 2027, here is where to find the series:

  • Streaming home: The first two films and the “Knuckles” spin-off series are currently streaming on Paramount+.
  • Digital access: “Sonic the Hedgehog 3” is available for digital purchase on platforms like Amazon and Apple TV following its record-breaking winter run.
  • Theatrical future: Mark your calendars for March 19, 2027, as “Sonic 4” is confirmed as a theatrical-exclusive release.
  • Bonus: Paramount also teased an untitled Sonic universe “event” film currently slated for December 22, 2028.

Who we are

At CableTV.com, we’re not just entertainment experts—we’re also fans just like you. So while our first priority is reporting the best and most accurate information about TV providers and streaming services, we also love talking about our favorite shows and movies. So you can count on us to share our honest opinions and unbridled enthusiasm. For more on our process, check out our Editorial Policy.

More industry news for TV fans

  • Disney Just Renewed 4 Shows, But Cancelled a Classic
  • New on Prime Video March 2026
  • What To Watch on Prime Video

item.title: ‘Bridgerton’ Breaks the Mold With Season 5 Leading Couple
item.dc:creator: CableTV.com
item.description: Gentle Readers: We finally know the ‘Bridgerton’ Season 5 leads. See the full announcement, their story so far, and why this twist isn’t a surprise.
item.content:encoded:
Season 5 will adapt “When He Was Wicked” by Julia Quinn

As long-time “Bridgerton” fans will know, each season focuses on a different one of Julia Quinn’s popular romance novels. The fifth book, “To Sir Phillip, With Love,” focuses on the love story between Eloise Bridgerton and Sir Phillip. However, Netflix is skipping over Eloise’s story (for now!) and focusing on book six: “When He Was Wicked.” That means that when “Bridgerton” returns for its next season, we’ll finally see the love story play out between Francesca Bridgerton (Hannah Dodd) and her husband’s cousin, Michaela Stirling (Masali Baduza). Production is just starting, so it’ll be a while before we see our favorite Regency-era family again. But while we wait, let’s go over what this means for the future of the show.

Wait, can they do that?

Some book fans are surprised or even angry to see Francesca get the spotlight so soon, but I’m not surprised by the decision. For one thing, Netflix already pulled this move once: In the books, Benedict gets married before Colin, but their order is flipped in the show. Both Benedict and Colin got their respective time in the TV show’s spotlight, so I don’t think that Eloise fans need to be worried. She’ll get her own time, likely in Season 6. It’s also not surprising to see Francesca become a leading lady due to how much attention the story has given her in the last two seasons. While the A-plot focused on Polin and Benophie, Francesca stole the show in her B-plot: falling in love with an Earl, getting married, learning important biology lessons, befriending her enemy, and watching her whole life fall apart. Eloise has had much less to do in comparison. Netflix might as well have spelled out “FRANCESCA IS NEXT” in Queen Charlotte’s voluminous wig.

Where we left off (spoilers!)

It’d be pretty harsh if Francesca had to wait a whole extra season for her happily ever after, considering what she went through in Season 4. When we last saw Francesca, she was mourning the loss of her husband, John Stirling, aka the Earl of Kilmartin. He died suddenly, and without an heir, putting Francesca’s inheritance into question. It took a lot for Francesca to find and marry John—both were quiet introverts, and very unlike the rest of the characters we’ve seen on the show—and Francesca claimed that she would never find another “great love.” … Except that wouldn’t make for a very interesting fourth season. Instead, thanks to the books, we know that she’s destined to fall in love with her late husband’s cousin as they mourn his loss together. Michaela (gender-bent from Michael in the books) first showed up at the end of Season 3 and spent most of Season 4 making Francesca’s life miserable. While Michaela was visiting John, the two cousins would stay up all night talking and celebrating, which clashed with Francesca’s vision of her quiet domestic life. But the two women eventually found common ground and became friends just before John’s death. Francesca and Michaela mourned John together until Francesca asked Michaela to extend her trip to London and stay close to her. Michaela left town without a word—perhaps overcome by some forbidden feelings? Only Lady Whistledown (the new one, I should add) can say. I’m excited to see how “When He Was Wicked” plays out in the show. Or rather: “When She Was Wicked.”

Who we are

At CableTV.com, we’re not just entertainment experts—we’re also fans just like you. So while our first priority is reporting the best and most accurate information about TV providers and streaming services, we also love talking about our favorite shows and movies. So you can count on us to share our honest opinions and unbridled enthusiasm. For more on our process, check out our Editorial Policy.

More industry news for TV fans

  • “Assassin’s Creed” Show Setting Revealed
  • “Hannah Montana” Returns to Disney+
  • Best Shows About Historical Women

item.title: You Won’t Believe the Video Game Film Adaptations Coming in 2026 and 2027
item.dc:creator: CableTV.com
item.description: We’ve researched every video game film adaptation coming soon so you don’t have to. See the full list of upcoming movies and TV shows for 2026 and 2027.
item.content:encoded:
✦ Edited and fact-checked by Randy Harward, our Entertainment Editor with 25 years covering entertainment and TV.

Level up: Every video game film and TV adaptation in 2026 and 2027

The video game movie curse is officially dead and buried. After the massive success of “The Last of Us” and “Fallout,” we have entered a new era where controllers are being traded for remote controls at record speeds. We are looking at a 2026-2027 calendar that is shaping up to be a prestige tentpole year, as many productions delayed by the 2023 Hollywood strikes finally hit our favorite streaming services. Whether you are a hardcore lore-police veteran or a newcomer just looking for a high-budget binge, we are tracking everything from Nintendo’s growing cinematic universe to Amazon’s massive fantasy bets. While you wait for these to drop, check out our list of the best Prime Video shows to binge to see how the bar has been raised.

“The Super Mario Galaxy Movie” (April 3, 2026)

Nintendo and Illumination have officially set April 3, 2026, for the return of everyone’s favorite plumbers. Following the billion-dollar success of the first film, this sequel is expected to expand the Super Mario Galaxy even further and introduce characters like Yoshi and Rosalina. It is a key part of Nintendo’s cinematic universe strategy that is making video game IP just as relevant as Marvel or DC.

“Street Fighter” (October 16, 2026)

Sony has scheduled the new live-action “Street Fighter” movie for October 16, 2026. After a few rocky attempts at this franchise in decades past, the focus this time is on source fidelity and high-octane martial arts choreography. We are seeing Sony (PlayStation Productions) keeping their IP closer to home to ensure that the lore-police are satisfied with the final product.

“Tomb Raider” (2027)

Phoebe Waller-Bridge is at the helm for the upcoming Amazon “Tomb Raider” series (starring Sophie Turner as Lara Croft), and the expectations are sky-high. This is part of a source-fidelity movement, where high-profile creators are tasked with modernizing classic icons while keeping their adventurous spirit intact.Pro tip: A new “Assassin’s Creed” adaptation from Netflix was just announced. Check out our new fan guide for the latest deets.

“The Legend of Zelda” (May 7, 2027)

The narrative surrounding the live-action “The Legend of Zelda” movie is the biggest storyline in Hollywood right now. The dominant debate among fans centers on how Nintendo will handle Link (Benjamin Evan Ainsworth) as a talking protagonist (he’s mostly mum in the game). We expect Nintendo to handle this with extreme care as they transition from a gaming company to a full-blown transmedia powerhouse.

“Death Stranding” (2027)

Game creator Hideo Kojima is working directly with A24 to bring “Death Stranding” to the big screen. This project perfectly illustrates the new prestige standard, where the game’s original creator acts as an executive producer. This isn’t just a movie; it’s an attempt to redefine what a video game adaptation can be, moving past action tropes into the realm of high-concept sci-fi.

“God of War” (late 2027)

Amazon is aggressively bidding for “hardcore” RPG titles, and “God of War” is the crown jewel of that strategy. We are hearing that the budget for this series is pushing well past the nine-digit mark to ensure the Norse realms look as epic as they did on the PlayStation 5. The insider “hopium” for this one is high—fans are desperate for the showrunners to capture the emotional father-son core between Kratos (Ryan Hurst) and Atreus (Callum Vinson) rather than just focusing on the spectacle.

“Warhammer 40,000” (2027/2028)

Henry Cavill is leading the charge on this massive adaptation for Prime Video, ensuring “lore accuracy” is the top priority. We know that the media landscape is shifting toward this kind of “transmedia consolidation,” where the creators are deeply involved in the production. This series aims to bring the grimdark future of the 41st millennium to life with the kind of prestige storytelling we previously only saw in cinema.

Video game film adaptations FAQ

What are the upcoming video game adaptations for 2026?

The 2026 calendar includes "The Super Mario Bros. Movie 2" (April 3), "Street Fighter" (October 16).

Is there a 'Legend of Zelda' movie coming out?

Nintendo has confirmed a live-action "The Legend of Zelda" movie is in development with director Wes Ball and Sony Pictures. While a firm 2026 release is not yet official, industry buzz suggests it is a priority for Nintendo’s new transmedia push.

Why are so many video game movies being made now?

Following the success of "The Last of Us" and "The Super Mario Bros. Movie," studios view video game IP as being as valuable as Marvel or DC. The shift toward hiring original game creators as producers has also led to higher quality and better fan reception.

What to binge next

  • Best Prime Video Shows to Binge
  • Upcoming Video Game Releases
  • Upcoming Anime Simulcasts
CableTV.com editors created this article using original analysis and proprietary inputs, with AI tools assisting in drafting. The final content was reviewed and edited for accuracy and quality.

item.title: The New ‘Assassin’s Creed’ Has Been Thinking a Lot About the Roman Empire
item.dc:creator: CableTV.com
item.description: It’s official: Netflix’s ‘Assassin’s Creed’ series is headed to a new time and place. See the full cast list and details from the latest press release here.
item.content:encoded:
Good news if ‘Assassin’s Creed’ is your Roman Empire

Netflix recently broke its silence on the upcoming live-action “Assassin’s Creed” series. Fans have long been wondering which game the show would adapt, and we finally have an answer: None of them. Each “Assassin’s Creed” game takes place in a different historical setting, but the Netflix show will take place in one we’ve never seen before: the Roman Empire, 64 AD. The “Assassin’s Creed” show will tell an original story rather than rehashing anything you’ve seen in the games before. But if you want to play one of the games with a similar vibe, you might want to check out “Assassin’s Creed Origins” (Egypt, 49 BC) or “Assassin’s Creed Odyssey” (Ancient Greece, 431 BC).

‘Assassin’s Creed’ Netflix cast

While we don’t know much about the characters in the upcoming “Assassin’s Creed” show, we do know a bit about the actors playing them. Here are all of the cast members who have been revealed so far. Series regulars:

  • Lola Petticrew
  • Toby Wallace
  • Zachary Hart
  • Laura Marcus
  • Tanzyn Crawford
  • Nabhaan Rizwan
  • Claes Bang

Recurring cast:

  • Noomi Rapace
  • Ramzy Bedia
  • Sean Harris
  • Corrado Invernizzi
  • Sandra Guldberg-Kampp
  • Youssef Kerkour
  • Mirren Mack
  • Louis McCartney

‘Assassin’s Creed’ live-action release date

Unfortunately, we don’t know exactly when “Assassin’s Creed” will come to Netflix. Filming only just began in Rome, Italy, so it’ll be a while before the finished project comes to our screens.

Who we are

At CableTV.com, we’re not just entertainment experts—we’re also fans just like you. So while our first priority is reporting the best and most accurate information about TV providers and streaming services, we also love talking about our favorite shows and movies. So you can count on us to share our honest opinions and unbridled enthusiasm. For more on our process, check out our Editorial Policy.

More industry news for TV fans

  • How to Watch ‘One Piece’ Season 2
  • Netflix Backs Off WBD Deal
  • How to Watch ‘Young Sherlock‘
